meaning: adj.未持续的,不受支持的,未保持的
examples: Sometimes VT can last for 30 seconds or less (unsustained), and it might not cause any symptoms, although it causes inefficient heartbeats.
All evidence to date suggests that isolated instances of limited, unsustained human-to-human transmission can be expected from avian influenza viruses in humans.
The Pruning technology was used to make the network architecture unsustained growth, and avoid the fitting and the training phenomenon, thus, it has guaranteed system's pan-ability.
